original_hypothesis.title: Stale canOffboard Flag Allows Immediate Re-Offboarding After Re-Onboarding, Permanently Pausing PSM Redemptions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a lending term is offboarded, `canOffboard[term]` is set to true and `nOffboardingsInProgress` is incremented. If the term is re-onboarded by governance (via addGauge) before cleanup() is called, `canOffboard[term]` persists as true. An attacker can immediately call `offboard(term)` again without any new poll or quorum, bypassing the democratic offboarding process. This double-increments `nOffboardingsInProgress` but cleanup() can only decrement it once (setting `canOffboard[term] = false`), leaving a permanent residual that keeps PSM redemptions paused.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The canOffboard mapping is never reset when a term is re-onboarded, creating a state inconsistency. Combined with the nOffboardingsInProgress counter tracking PSM pause state, this creates a permanent DoS on PSM redemptions.

proof_of_signal.pattern: offboard() only checks canOffboard[term] which persists from previous offboarding cycle; no check that the term is currently in an active offboarding round

preconditions: A term was previously offboarded (canOffboard = true)
Governance re-onboarded the term without cleanup being called first
Attacker can call offboard() - no special roles needed

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: nOffboardingsInProgress incremented in offboard() but only decremented in cleanup() which sets canOffboard=false, preventing further decrements
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: offboard(term) called with stale canOffboard[term]=true after re-onboarding
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Permanent PSM redemption pause locks all CREDIT holders from redeeming for peg tokens; attacker profits by shorting CREDIT or holding collateral positions

economic_questions: What is the total value locked in PSM that becomes irredeemable?
Can governance recover from permanent nOffboardingsInProgress elevation?
Is there a path to trigger cleanup twice to fix the counter?
validation_requirements: Simulate: offboard term -> re-onboard -> offboard again -> verify nOffboardingsInProgress is 2
Call cleanup -> verify nOffboardingsInProgress is 1 and canOffboard is false
Verify no path to decrement nOffboardingsInProgress further

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: GUILD tokens needed for initial offboard quorum + gas
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: entire PSM peg token balance becomes locked
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Dependent on attacker's ability to profit from PSM disruption

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after governance re-onboards a previously offboarded term
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Systemic: all CREDIT holders unable to redeem via PSM

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All CREDIT holders wanting to redeem via PSM
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Unable to redeem CREDIT for peg tokens

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if any terms have been re-onboarded without cleanup in deployment

validation_result.mechanism: stale canOffboard flag allows re-offboarding without new poll, permanently corrupting nOffboardingsInProgress counter
validation_result.impact_summary: A re-onboarded term can be immediately offboarded again without a new quorum poll, permanently inflating nOffboardingsInProgress and keeping PSM redemptions paused until governance manually intervenes.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The vulnerability exists because `canOffboard[term]` is only set to `false` in `cleanup()`, not when a term is re-onboarded via `addGauge()`. After a term is offboarded (canOffboard = true, nOffboardingsInProgress incremented), if governance re-onboards it before cleanup is called (e.g., because loans are still outstanding), the stale `canOffboard[term] = true` allows anyone to immediately call `offboard(term)` again. This increments `nOffboardingsInProgress` a second time. When `cleanup()` is eventually called, it can only decrement `nOffboardingsInProgress` once and sets `canOffboard[term] = false`. The counter is permanently elevated by 1, and PSM redemptions can never be unpaused through the normal `cleanup()` path.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The vulnerability is clearly visible in the code. `offboard()` at LendingTermOffboarding.sol only checks `canOffboard[term]` (a stale flag) and has no check that a fresh poll was conducted for the current onboarding cycle. `_addGauge()` in ERC20Gauges.sol does not interact with LendingTermOffboarding to reset `canOffboard`. `cleanup()` is the only function that sets `canOffboard[term] = false` and decrements `nOffboardingsInProgress`, but it can only be called once per offboarding cycle.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: offboard() only checks canOffboard[term] which persists from a previous offboarding cycle. After re-onboarding (addGauge), canOffboard is not reset, so offboard() can be called again without a new poll, double-incrementing nOffboardingsInProgress.

cleanup() can only decrement nOffboardingsInProgress once per term (since it sets canOffboard[term] = false). If offboard() was called twice due to re-onboarding without cleanup, the counter is permanently elevated.
_addGauge (called during re-onboarding) does not interact with LendingTermOffboarding to reset canOffboard[term]. The re-onboarding path has no awareness of the offboarding state.

calling_sequence: 1. supportOffboard() sets canOffboard[term] = true when quorum reached
2. offboard(term) called: canOffboard[term] check passes, removeGauge(term) deprecates gauge, nOffboardingsInProgress incremented to 1
3. Governance re-onboards term via addGauge(_type, term): _deprecatedGauges.remove(term) restores gauge, but canOffboard[term] remains true in LendingTermOffboarding
4. Attacker calls offboard(term) again: canOffboard[term] is still true, removeGauge(term) succeeds since gauge is live again, nOffboardingsInProgress incremented to 2
5. cleanup(term) called: nOffboardingsInProgress decremented to 1, canOffboard[term] set to false
6. nOffboardingsInProgress is permanently stuck at 1, PSM redemptions cannot be unpaused through normal cleanup path
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The root cause is at LendingTermOffboarding.sol:155 where `require(canOffboard[term])` is the only check in offboard(). This flag is never reset during re-onboarding (ERC20Gauges._addGauge at line 356-384 has no interaction with LendingTermOffboarding). The counter corruption occurs at line 163 where `nOffboardingsInProgress++` happens a second time for the same logical offboarding process. cleanup() at line 190 can only do `--nOffboardingsInProgress` once since it sets `canOffboard[term] = false` at line 196.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that are relevant to this vulnerability. The vulnerability is inherent in the contract logic.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario is fully valid: (1) A poll is created and reaches quorum for term T. (2) offboard(T) is called - nOffboardingsInProgress = 1, PSM paused. (3) Governance re-onboards T via LendingTermOnboarding proposal executing addGauge(). (4) Attacker calls offboard(T) - succeeds because canOffboard[T] is still true. removeGauge works because T was re-added as live gauge. nOffboardingsInProgress = 2. (5) After all loans close, cleanup(T) is called: nOffboardingsInProgress = 1, canOffboard[T] = false. (6) PSM redemptions stay paused forever through normal paths. This can be repeated to inflate the counter arbitrarily.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL - The attack permanently corrupts the nOffboardingsInProgress counter, preventing PSM redemptions from being unpaused through the normal cleanup() mechanism. This effectively creates a permanent denial of service on CREDIT redemptions via the PSM, impacting all CREDIT holders. While governance can manually call SimplePSM.setRedemptionsPaused(false), this requires active governance intervention and the counter remains corrupted, meaning any future legitimate offboarding/cleanup cycle will also fail to properly unpause. Each repetition of the attack adds +1 to the permanent counter inflation.
recommended_mitigations: Reset canOffboard[term] = false in addGauge() when a term is re-onboarded, by having GuildToken.addGauge() call a callback on LendingTermOffboarding

Add a check in offboard() that the term is currently a live gauge: require(GuildToken(guildToken).isGauge(term)), and verify the poll is from a recent block (lastPollBlock[term] + POLL_DURATION_BLOCKS >= block.number)
Track offboarding round IDs per term to ensure offboard() can only execute once per poll round
Add a governance-callable function to manually decrement nOffboardingsInProgress to recover from corrupted state
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy full ECG system with a LendingTerm, 2. Have GUILD holders reach offboarding quorum via proposeOffboard() and supportOffboard(), 3. Call offboard(term), verify nOffboardingsInProgress = 1 and PSM paused, 4. Execute governance proposal to re-onboard the term via addGauge(), 5. Call offboard(term) again without any new poll, verify it succeeds and nOffboardingsInProgress = 2, 6. Close all loans and call cleanup(term), verify nOffboardingsInProgress = 1 and canOffboard = false, 7. Verify PSM redemptions remain paused and there's no path to reach nOffboardingsInProgress = 0
uncertainties: Whether governance can practically recover by calling SimplePSM.setRedemptionsPaused(false) directly - this is possible but leaves nOffboardingsInProgress corrupted
Whether the LendingTermOffboarding contract actually has the GOVERNOR role needed to call SimplePSM.setRedemptionsPaused() - if not, the PSM pause/unpause in offboard/cleanup would revert
additional_testing_needed: Verify the access control setup: does LendingTermOffboarding have CoreRoles.GOVERNOR to call SimplePSM.setRedemptionsPaused?
Test whether multiple terms can be exploited simultaneously to inflate the counter by more than 1
Test governance recovery path: can governance directly call setRedemptionsPaused(false) while nOffboardingsInProgress > 0?
